category: Accion Trust by Design Fintech Challenge / student_partner_track

rules.title: Submission must be made by an eligible in-market fintech or financial-services partner.
Solution must be live with active customers at submission time.
Trust layer MVP covers transparency, fraud prompts, consent, complaint resolution, and harm mitigation.
Gender-disaggregated metric schema and reporting outputs are ready for partner data.
All 42 application-guide questions are mapped to draft answers and partner input fields.
WAIBAv dry-run and authorized final-submit playbook are prepared with a legal authority gate.

milestones.title: V trust engine and metric validation
Vue reporting demo and submission packet

integrations.name: Trust engine, gender metrics, validation, and fintech adapters
Trust reporting cockpit for partner review
External Microsoft Forms preparation and dry-run receipts
Reusable challenge manifest and evidence renderers

dimensions.label: Traction & Market Validation
dimensions.evidence: Partner checklist requires active users, retention, and transaction evidence.
dimensions.gap: Insert partner-specific live traction before final submit.

dimensions.label: Harm Reduction & Trust Signal
dimensions.evidence: Trust layer MVP and V trust engine.
dimensions.gap: Attach partner baseline and after metrics.

dimensions.label: Gender-Disaggregated Data
dimensions.evidence: Metric schema, report JSON, and application draft.
dimensions.gap: Replace synthetic sample with partner export.

dimensions.label: Regulatory & Compliance Readiness
dimensions.evidence: Partner diligence, privacy, consent, KYC/AML, and complaint gates.
dimensions.gap: Partner counsel must approve jurisdiction-specific statements.

dimensions.label: Clarity & Coherence
dimensions.evidence: Submission packet, application draft, and Vue report.
dimensions.gap: Final brand names must match partner legal entity.

competitors.name: Live fintech with built-in trust feature
competitors.strength: Already has customers and real outcomes.
competitors.weakness: May have weak explainability or no reusable trust instrumentation.
competitors.counter: Position as measurable trust infrastructure embedded inside the partner journey.

competitors.name: Fraud analytics provider
competitors.strength: Strong risk signal and measurable loss reduction.
competitors.weakness: Often less gender-responsive and less customer-facing.
competitors.counter: Show trust prompts, consent, complaint loops, and women-specific outcomes.

competitors.name: Financial education app
competitors.strength: Easy trust narrative and customer empathy.
competitors.weakness: May not affect live financial-product decisions.
competitors.counter: Tie education to transaction, repayment, complaint, and risk moments.

next_moves: Secure one eligible fintech partner that is in-market, post-revenue, and serving women customers.
Import partner GDD export into the V trust engine and replace synthetic evidence.
Run the Microsoft Forms workflow in an authorized representative session.
Prepare Stage 2 interview proof: demo, metric lineage, privacy posture, and partner authority.

proof_sprint.name: Accion Trust by Design express proof sprint
proof_sprint.objective: Convert the student partner track package into a partner-authorized Accion submission with real traction, gender-disaggregated metrics, and trust outcomes.

steps.title: Partner legal and metric intake
steps.output: Approved facts, GDD export, authority confirmation

win_conditions: No claim depends on synthetic data in the final submitted form.
The applicant has authority to bind the fintech to official rules.
Submission keeps privacy, consent, and harm-reduction safeguards explicit.

founder_proof.title: Founder and dogfood proof
founder_proof.public_summary: Mexico-based software architecture and automation founder with 15+ years across enterprise integrations, fiscal software, ERP workflows, AI automation, cloud delivery, and technical leadership.
founder_proof.private_boundary: Fiscal IDs, phone, email, CSD keys, legal documents, bank data, and raw OCR records stay private and are referenced only through redacted manifests or checksums.

signals.label: Senior architecture and technical leadership
signals.note: Public CV material supports leadership, architecture, and delivery claims.

signals.label: AI, automation, and Gemini experience
signals.note: Skills file lists AI/LLM integrations, Gemini, scraping, and automation.

signals.label: Fiscal software and enterprise integrations
signals.note: Selected projects include Mexican electronic billing and integration work.

signals.label: Operational profile database
signals.note: Used only to derive public-safe summaries and private evidence boundaries.

dogfood_cases.label: FacturaMX SaaS
dogfood_cases.note: AI Studio app exists, but live access requires Google sign-in handoff.

dogfood_cases.label: Odoo automation workspace
dogfood_cases.note: WAIBAv-compatible Odoo automation utilities and playbooks exist.

dogfood_cases.label: Odoo RPP tutorial and QA evidence
dogfood_cases.note: Contains product/tutorial automation outputs useful as redacted case-study material.

dogfood_cases.label: AI tutorial maker
dogfood_cases.note: Shows education and workflow automation product direction.

dogfood_cases.label: Local agent ecosystem
dogfood_cases.note: Reusable ingestion, automation, and accountable-agent runtimes are available.
